alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed preference model can compare two arbitrarily given design sequences with almost 90% accuracy against random design sequences.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The autoregressive transformer model is capable of autocompleting a volumetric design sequence from a partial design sequence.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same capability claim for the autoregressive transformer as in the preprint.

alignments.rationale: Both documents state the same contribution regarding encoding design knowledge with transformer-based models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our deep learning-based approach achieved a predictive accuracy of 52%, outperforming a previously published method's accuracy of 45%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our deep learning-based approach achieved a predictive accuracy of 52%, outperforming a previously published method's accuracy of 45%.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance comparison and numbers as the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The agreement between the predicted scores of our method and the visual scores was good, while the previous method obtained only moderate agreement.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The agreement between the predicted scores of our method and the visual scores was good, while the previous method obtained only moderate agreement.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same claim about agreement quality compared to the prior method.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method possesses the capability to compute the percentage of emphysema involvement per lung in addition to categorical severity scores.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method possesses the capability to compute the percentage of emphysema involvement per lung in addition to categorical severity scores.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this capability claim verbatim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method extends its predictive capabilities beyond centrilobular emphysema to include paraseptal emphysema subtypes.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same claim about extending to paraseptal emphysema subtypes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The automated labeling approach significantly improves the performance of image classification for radiographic knee diagnosis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The automated labeling approach significantly improves the performance of image classification for radiographic knee diagnosis.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the main result claim of improved classification performance with the automated labeling approach.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The final image classification model achieved a higher weighted average AUC (WAUC: 0.903) compared to the baseline model (WAUC=0.857).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The final image classification model achieved a higher weighted average AUC (WA-AUC 0.903) compared to the baseline model (WA-AUC = 0.857).

alignments.pub_claim_text: Statistical tests show that the improvement in classification performance is significant for normal, abnormal, and WA-AUC categories.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach allows for facilitating patient care and curation of large knee datasets.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: QCNNs are prone to overfitting, which negatively impacts their performance on unseen datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: QCNNs are prone to overfitting, which negatively impacts their performance on unseen datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the conclusion about QCNNs' tendency to overfit and the negative effect on unseen data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The decrease in success probability due to neuron dropout highlights the crucial role of entanglement in QCNNs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The decrease in success probability due to neuron dropout highlights the crucial role of entanglement in QCNNs and their vulnerability to entanglement loss.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A proposed parameter adaptation method is computationally efficient and successfully mitigates overfitting in QCNNs.

alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core claim about gaps/opportunities but adds clarification by explicitly calling out unresolved issues and challenges.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of physics-informed capabilities in reinforcement learning algorithms holds great potential for increasing their physical plausibility, precision, data efficiency, and applicability in real-world scenarios. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Spark tuning framework effectively addresses the issues of limited functionality, high overhead, and inefficient search in auto-tuning techniques. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Spark tuning framework effectively addresses the issues of limited functionality, high overhead, and inefficient search in auto-tuning techniques. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework introduces a generalized tuning formulation that supports multiple tuning goals and constraints. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Empirical results demonstrate that the framework saves an average of 57.00% memory cost and 34.93% CPU cost on 25K in-production tasks within 20 iterations. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework utilizes online evaluations to tune parameters during actual job executions, thereby avoiding high overhead from additional offline evaluations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Three innovative techniques, including adaptive sub-space generation, approximate gradient descent, and a meta-learning method, are employed to accelerate the search process. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Defrag is the first pathway-inference method to utilize a neural network approach. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method demonstrates promising results comparable in efficiency to traditional scores across ten datasets of real-world interest.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method is particularly effective for under-represented categories in semantic scene segmentation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the proposed SSA has been compared with that of genetic algorithms (GAs), particle swarm optimization (PSO), and ant colony optimization (ACO).

alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the comparative performance claim; the inclusion of 'continuous' before ACO is a minor specification and does not change the claim meaningfully.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed SSA demonstrates generally higher performance than the other algorithms tested.

alignments.rationale: The publication includes both the general superiority claim (Q1) and a quantitative result (Q2). P2 corresponds to the general performance claim; although Q2 is more specific numeric result, the overall message of improved performance is unchanged.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The YCB-Ev dataset introduces synchronized RGB-D frames and event data for evaluating 6DoF object pose estimation algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim, so there is no meaningful semantic, scope, or confidence change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset provides ground truth 6DoF object poses for 21 YCB objects, enabling cross-dataset algorithm performance evaluation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset provides ground truth 6DoF object poses for 21 YCB objects, enabling cross-dataset algorithm performance evaluation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The YCB-Ev dataset is the first to provide ground truth 6DoF pose data for event streams.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The YCB-Ev dataset is the first to provide ground truth 6DoF pose data for event streams.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the novelty claim identically, so it is unchanged.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The majority of LLMs tested have a strong propensity to output stereotypes in the Indian context.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same empirical finding without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GPT-2, GPT-2 Large, and GPT 3.5 have a particularly high propensity for preferring stereotypical outputs as a percent of all sentences for the axes of caste (63-79%) and religion (69-72%).
alignments.pub_claim_text: GPT-2, GPT-2 Large, and GPT 3.5 have a particularly high propensity for preferring stereotypical outputs as a percent of all sentences for the axes of caste (63–79%) and religion (69–72%).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings highlight the need for including more diverse voices when researching fairness in AI and evaluating LLMs.

alignments.rationale: The conclusion urging inclusion of diverse voices is preserved in the publication without substantive change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Using a subspace of the neural network’s representation can recover performance.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The synthetic minority oversampling technique (SMOTE) improves the performance of machine learning models.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Models are evaluated using decision support metrics such as precision, recall, F1-Score, and accuracy.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Differentiable indirection is a novel learned primitive that employs differentiable multi-scale lookup tables as a substitute for traditional compute and data operations across the graphics pipeline. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Differentiable indirection demonstrates flexibility on a number of graphics tasks, including geometric and image representation, texture mapping, shading, and radiance field representation. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Background features play a more critical role than foreground features in image-level cross-domain alignment. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed background-focused distribution alignment (BFDA) framework effectively trains domain adaptive one-stage pedestrian detectors. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed background-focused distribution alignment (BFDA) framework significantly enhances cross-domain pedestrian detection performance compared to mainstream domain adaptation technologies. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: MHVAE synthesizes missing images from various modalities using a deep hierarchical variational auto-encoder. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The advantage of using a hierarchical latent representation and a principled probabilistic fusion operation is demonstrated. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Artificial intelligence, particularly deep learning, has great potential for detecting hidden tobacco advertising.
alignments.rationale: High-level conclusion about AI potential is identically stated in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model can achieve 74% accuracy for images and 98% for text in detecting smoking cases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieves 74% accuracy for images and 98% for text.
alignments.rationale: Exact performance figures are preserved in the publication; claim content is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system integrates the possibility of expert intervention in the form of human reinforcement.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system allows for expert intervention through human reinforcement.
alignments.rationale: Same claim about enabling expert/human reinforcement intervention appears in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using pre-trained multimodal, image, and text processing models makes it possible to detect smoking in different media even with few training data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of pre-trained multimodal models enables detection of smoking in different media with little training data.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim that pre-trained multimodal models enable detection with limited training data; meaning unchanged.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed integrated text and image processing model can detect smoking cases in both textual and visual formats.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes an explicit contribution statement that the integrated model detects both text and image cases; while implied by preprint results, this explicit formulation does not appear as a standalone claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Uncertainty-aware Pseudo Label guided (UPL) SFDA method improves medical image segmentation performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Uncertainty-aware Pseudo Label guided (UPL) SFDA method improves average Dice scores by 5.54, 5.01, and 6.89 percentage points for three medical image segmentation tasks compared to the baseline. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same overall claim but adds exact numerical improvements, clarifying the degree of improvement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: UPL-SFDA improved the average Dice by 5.54, 5.01, and 6.89 percentage points for three different segmentation tasks compared to the baseline. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Uncertainty-aware Pseudo Label guided (UPL) SFDA method improves average Dice scores by 5.54, 5.01, and 6.89 percentage points for three medical image segmentation tasks compared to the baseline. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical result is reproduced verbatim in the publication with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: UPL-SFDA outperformed several state-of-the-art SFDA methods in medical image segmentation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: UPL-SFDA outperforms several state-of-the-art Source-Free Domain Adaptation methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert outperforming SOTA SFDA methods with equivalent meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Target Domain Growing (TDG) strategy enhances the diversity of predictions in the target domain. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Target Domain Growing (TDG) strategy enhances the diversity of predictions in the target domain by duplicating the pre-trained model's prediction head with perturbations. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an implementation detail (duplicating head with perturbations), clarifying how TDG increases diversity without changing the claimed effect.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Twice Forward pass Supervision (TFS) strategy uses reliable pseudo labels to supervise predictions in subsequent forward passes. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Twice Forward pass Supervision (TFS) strategy uses reliable pseudo labels from one forward pass to supervise predictions in the next forward pass. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same mechanism (using pseudo labels from one pass to supervise the next) with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pre-training on image-text pairs significantly improves the performance of a visual embedder trained for the logo retrieval task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Pre-training on image-text pairs significantly improves the performance of a visual embedder trained for the logo retrieval task, especially for more text-dominant classes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication qualifies the improvement as particularly strong for text-dominant classes, clarifying and somewhat narrowing the original general claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed ProxyNCAHN++ loss function sets new state-of-the-art performance on five public logo datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method sets new state-of-the-art on five public logo datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same SOTA result across the five datasets, matching the preprint's claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fine-tuning a vision backbone pre-trained on image-text data achieves 98.6% recall@1, outperforming the 97.6% recall@1 achieved by pre-training on Imagenet1K. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fine-tuning a vision backbone pre-trained on image-text data achieves 98.6% recall@1, significantly improving performance over pre-training on Imagenet1K. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical comparison is preserved and emphasized in the publication with equivalent meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new method provides a 3.5% zero-shot recall@1 improvement on LogoDet3K test and improvements on other datasets as well. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the stated 3.5% zero-shot recall@1 improvement or analogous zero-shot quantitative results, indicating the claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fine-tuned large language models can generate accurate, personalized impressions for whole-body PET reports. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Large language models can create clinically useful impressions for PET reporting. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core conclusion that LLMs produce useful PET impressions but frames them as "clinically useful" rather than explicitly "accurate, personalized," which is a clarification in emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fine-tuned PEGASUS model was selected as the top LLM based on evaluation metrics that showed high correlations with physician preferences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Personalized impressions generated by PEGASUS were comparable in overall utility to impressions dictated by other physicians. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's stronger claim (selection as top LLM via correlated metrics) is softened in the publication to indicating PEGASUS-generated impressions are comparable in utility, a tempering of the original assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 89% of PEGASUS-generated impressions were considered clinically acceptable by physicians. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: 89% of LLM impressions generated in the physicians' own style were considered clinically acceptable. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The 89% clinically acceptable result is reproduced in the publication with consistent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Personalized impressions generated by PEGASUS were rated comparably in overall utility to impressions dictated by other physicians. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Personalized impressions generated by PEGASUS were comparable in overall utility to impressions dictated by other physicians. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same comparison claim about PEGASUS-personalized impressions being comparable in utility to human impressions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm enhances the robustness and generalizability of ANN controllers by introducing morphological variations during the evolutionary training process. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study proposes an algorithm that enhances the robustness and generalizability of artificial neural network controllers by introducing morphological variations during the evolutionary training process. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Generalist controllers can handle a wide range of morphological variations without needing information about their morphologies or adaptation of their parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results demonstrate a trade-off between specialist and generalist controllers, with generalists able to control a range of morphological variations but underperforming on specific morphologies compared to specialists. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same finding that generalists handle a range of morphologies but may underperform relative to specialists, matching the preprint's result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental analysis demonstrates a trade-off between specialist and generalist controllers, with generalists underperforming on specific morphologies compared to specialists. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results demonstrate a trade-off between specialist and generalist controllers, with generalists able to control a range of morphological variations but underperforming on specific morphologies compared to specialists. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same experimental finding about the specialist-generalist trade-off with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This research addresses the limited understanding of robustness and generalizability in evolved ANNs. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This research addresses the limited understanding of robustness and generalizability in evolved artificial neural networks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication state the same contribution/aim regarding the limited understanding of robustness and generalizability in evolved ANNs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The stochastic deep Koopman (SDK) framework surpasses other popular data-driven models in accuracy when predicting stagewise product quality within multistage manufacturing systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The stochastic deep Koopman (SDK) framework surpasses other popular data-driven models in accuracy when predicting stagewise product quality within multistage manufacturing systems. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The unique linear propagation property in the stochastic latent space of SDK enables traceability for quality evolution throughout the manufacturing process. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The unique linear propagation property in the stochastic latent space of SDK enables traceability for quality evolution throughout the manufacturing process. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion about the SDK latent space enabling traceability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed SDK framework requires minimal knowledge of the underlying physics of production lines. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed SDK framework requires minimal knowledge of the underlying physics of production lines. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SDK framework serves as a virtual metrology tool that can be applied to various multistage manufacturing systems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SDK framework serves as a virtual metrology tool that can be applied to various multistage manufacturing systems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same claim that SDK can serve as a virtual metrology tool across systems.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Drone Aware Collision Management (DACM) methodology is capable of determining and executing a variety of time-optimal evasive collision avoidance maneuvers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Drone Aware Collision Management (DACM) methodology has been demonstrated to effectively avoid mid-air collisions (MAC) between UAVs and manned aeroplanes.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core idea of DACM avoiding collisions but drops the specific assertion about determining/executing time-optimal evasive maneuvers and their variety, so the claim is revised and effectively narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DACM methodology has been demonstrated through extensive simulations and real-world field tests to effectively avoid mid-air collisions between UAVs and manned aeroplanes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Drone Aware Collision Management (DACM) methodology has been demonstrated to effectively avoid mid-air collisions (MAC) between UAVs and manned aeroplanes.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The reliable decision-making abilities of DACM can allow for the establishment of MAC standards for UAVs without introducing new collision risks during evasive manoeuvres.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Training the model partially can accelerate the training process and efficiently utilize resources on-device. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed massively parallel computer algorithm discovers an unprecedented number of continued fraction formulas for fundamental mathematical constants. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed multimodal approach using TwHIN-BERT and VGG16 demonstrates valuable advantages over state-of-the-art methods for bot detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed multimodal approach using TwHIN-BERT and VGG16 demonstrates valuable advantages over state-of-the-art methods for bot detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is repeated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study is the first to employ only the user description field and images to detect bots in social media. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study is the first to employ only the user description field and images to detect bots in social media. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The novelty claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing methods for bot detection have substantial limitations, including tedious feature extraction and high computational resource requirements. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing methods for bot detection have substantial limitations, including tedious feature extraction and high computational resource requirements. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The described limitations are restated unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Language models based on transformers have been proven to be better than LSTMs for bot detection tasks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Language models based on transformers have been proven to be better than LSTMs for bot detection tasks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The comparative conclusion about transformers versus LSTMs is retained exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The training scheme of different foundation models dictates their effectiveness for tasks beyond speech recognition, especially for non-semantic speech tasks like emotion understanding.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The training scheme of different foundation models dictates their effectiveness for tasks beyond speech recognition.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same high-level claim but omits the preprint's explicit emphasis on non-semantic speech/emotion tasks, broadening the claim's scope while keeping confidence similar.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HuBERT-Large with a self-attention-based light-weight sequence model provides 4.6% improvement over the reported baseline.
alignments.pub_claim_text: HuBERT-Large with a self-attention-based light-weight sequence model provides a 4.6% improvement over the reported baseline in emotion understanding tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the specific task context (emotion understanding), making the result claim more specific (narrower) but otherwise consistent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Different shares of people perceive the same speech segment as a non-unanimous emotion depending on varying contexts such as languages and demographics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Speech emotion understanding is a perception task that varies across different contexts such as languages and demographics.
alignments.rationale: Both statements convey the same conclusion about perception of speech emotion varying with context; wording is slightly different but meaning is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LLMs are indeed prone to several human cognitive effects.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LLMs are indeed prone to several human cognitive effects.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result claim verbatim; no substantive change in meaning, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The priming, distance, SNARC, and size congruity effects were presented with GPT-3, while the anchoring effect is absent.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The priming, distance, SNARC, and size congruity effects were presented with GPT-3, while the anchoring effect is absent.
alignments.rationale: The specific empirical findings are carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We speculate on the possible reasons why GPT-3 exhibits these effects.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We speculate on the possible reasons why GPT-3 exhibits these effects.
alignments.rationale: The speculative explanatory claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generalization ability of deep convolutional neural networks can be improved by taking advantage of multi-layer and multi-scaled representations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generalization ability of deep convolutional neural networks (CNNs) can be improved by taking advantage of multilayer and multiscaled representations.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion with minor stylistic changes; meaning and scope are the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a novel objective function inspired by contrastive learning to constrain the extracted representations to remain invariant under distribution shifts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel objective function, inspired by contrastive learning, constrains the extracted representations to remain invariant under distribution shifts.
alignments.rationale: The methodological contribution is presented equivalently in the publication; no change in content or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model surpasses the performance of previous domain generalization methods and consistently produces competitive and state-of-the-art results across multiple datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our model surpasses the performance of previous domain generalization methods and consistently produces competitive and state-of-the-art results across multiple datasets.
alignments.rationale: The result claim is carried over verbatim; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning techniques can be applied for the classification of topological phases in leaky photonic lattices using limited measurement data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning techniques can be applied for the classification of topological phases in finite leaky photonic lattices using limited measurement data.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the qualifier 'finite', making the experimental setting more specific (narrower) but preserving the core contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach is based solely on bulk intensity measurements, exempting the need for complicated phase retrieval procedures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach is based solely on a single real-space bulk intensity image, exempt from complicated phase retrieval procedures.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies 'a single real-space bulk intensity image', tightening the claim to a more specific measurement type while keeping the same intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A fully connected neural network accurately determines topological properties from the output intensity distribution in dimerized waveguide arrays with leaky channels.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A fully connected neural network accurately determines topological properties from the output intensity distribution in dimerized waveguide arrays with leaky channels.
alignments.rationale: The result is reported identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method operates effectively after propagation of a spatially localized initial excitation at a finite distance, emulating realistic experimental conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method operates effectively after propagation of a spatially localized initial excitation at a finite distance, emulating realistic experimental conditions.
alignments.rationale: This operational/result claim is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CodeMark is a method that embeds user-defined imperceptible watermarks into code datasets to trace their usage in training neural code completion models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CodeMark is a method that embeds user-defined imperceptible watermarks into code datasets to trace their usage in training neural code completion models.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CodeMark preserves the exact functionality of the code data while keeping the changes covert against rule-breakers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CodeMark is based on adaptive semantic-preserving transformations that preserve the exact functionality of the code data.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the mechanism ('adaptive semantic-preserving transformations') and omits the explicit phrase about covert changes against rule-breakers, clarifying the technical approach.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CodeMark fulfills all desired properties of practical watermarks, including harmlessness to model accuracy, verifiability, robustness, and imperceptibility.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CodeMark has been validated to fulfill all desired properties of practical watermarks, including harmlessness to model accuracy, verifiability, robustness, and imperceptibility.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds that CodeMark 'has been validated' to meet the listed properties, which strengthens the confidence level while preserving the claim content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: UMMAFormer is a novel universal transformer framework for temporal forgery localization that predicts forgery segments with multimodal adaptation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: UMMAFormer is a novel universal transformer framework for temporal forgery localization that predicts forgery segments with multimodal adaptation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim, so there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Temporal Feature Abnormal Attention (TFAA) module enhances the detection of temporal differences through temporal feature reconstruction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Temporal Feature Abnormal Attention module enhances the detection of temporal differences through temporal feature reconstruction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The module and its claimed effect are described the same way in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method achieves state-of-the-art performance on benchmark datasets, including Lav-DF, TVIL, and Psynd, significantly outperforming previous methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method achieves state-of-the-art performance on benchmark datasets, including Lav-DF, TVIL, and Psynd. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the datasets and state-of-the-art claim but omits the explicit wording that it 'significantly outperforms' prior methods, reducing the strength of the result statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel Temporal Video Inpainting Localization (TVIL) dataset has been contributed specifically tailored for video inpainting scenes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state the dataset contribution present in the preprint, so the dataset claim is absent in the published version.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The existing research for forgery content detection has primarily focused on binary classification tasks of complete videos, limiting its applicability in industrial settings. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a new limitation/context statement not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NARF features detected more keypoints and resulted in a faster co-registration procedure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NARF features detected more keypoints and resulted in a faster co-registration procedure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy of the co-registration is similar across all combinations of keypoint detectors and features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy of the co-registration is similar across all combinations of keypoint detectors and features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same accuracy comparison claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work compares several strategies for fast alignment of point clouds from very different surveying geometries. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work compares several strategies for fast alignment of point clouds from very different surveying geometries. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is reproduced without change in the published paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Increasing the coverage of different road and traffic conditions and including corner cases in simulation-based scenario testing can improve the safety of Autonomous Vehicles (AVs). (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes method and generation of corner cases but does not explicitly state the general conclusion that increased coverage improves AV safety.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The creation of corner case scenarios including multiple agents is non-trivial. (certainty=high, type=limitation)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Probabilistic Lane Graphs (PLGs) are introduced to describe a finite set of lane positions and directions for vehicles. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DRL algorithms using deep neural networks can successfully approximate solutions in a non-linear three-variable model for a fishery without knowing or inferring a model for the process itself. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DRL algorithms using deep neural networks can successfully approximate solutions in a non-linear three-variable model for a fishery without knowing or attempting to infer a model for the process itself. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: i-Align is an interpretable KG alignment model that provides explanations for each alignment prediction while maintaining high alignment performance. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The high quality of a KG can be maintained during the curation process through the use of i-Align. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Trans-GE, a key component of i-Align, uses Edge-gated Attention to control information aggregation from neighboring entities. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of i-Align for aligning KGs and the quality of the generated explanations. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The geolocation data the RTK group was exposed to was less accurate on average than that of the control group.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The RTK group reported lower usability scores on all scales, with 5 out of 9 being significant, indicating that inaccurate data negatively predicts usability.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The GNSS group walked more than the RTK group, indicating a partial effect on exploration.
alignments.rationale: The result and its interpretation are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There was no significant effect on interaction time with the screen, indicating no specific relation between data accuracy and focus.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There was no significant effect on interaction time with the screen, indicating no specific relation between data accuracy and focus.

alignments.pub_claim_text: While RTK data did not improve the usability of location-based AR interfaces, the results allow for the assessment of the system’s overall usability as excellent.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing Collaborative Filtering methods suffer from noisy data issues that negatively impact recommendation quality.
alignments.rationale: The limitation/observation appears verbatim and retains the same meaning.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Sharpness-aware Collaborative Filtering (SharpCF) conducts adversarial training without extra computational cost over the base optimizer.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Adversarial training seeks model parameters that lie in neighborhoods around the optimal model parameters having uniformly low loss values, resulting in better generalizability.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that SharpCF achieves superior performance with almost zero additional computational cost compared to adversarial training.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed functional architecture integrates knowledge graphs with the linguistic capabilities of LLMs.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The architecture has been validated using real-world AI news data, demonstrating its ability to blend linguistic sophistication with factual rigor.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This research emphasizes the need for conversational AI systems that are efficient, transparent, and trustworthy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sparse modeling is widely applicable across various fields including statistics, physics, information sciences, neuroscience, and computational mathematics.

alignments.rationale: The specific claim about discovering predictive patterns is not present in the publication's listed claims and so appears to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The research on efficient recovery of high-dimensional sparse signals from a small number of observations has rapidly grown and become an intense area of study.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Conditions on the mutual coherence can lead to the uniqueness and recoverability of the sparsest solution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sparse modeling is directly related to various artificial vision tasks such as image denoising, segmentation, and object detection.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Mutual coherence is easy to compute and provides a lower bound to the spark.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Many greedy algorithms for sparse recovery seek a linear expansion of the unknown signal in terms of specific functions.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes this algorithmic detail that is not present in the preprint's claims, so it is considered an added claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent advances in numerical simulation methods combined with machine learning have improved the accuracy of weather forecasts.
alignments.rationale: The high-level conclusion is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy of weather forecasts decreases in complex terrains such as mountainous regions due to the use of large grids and simple machine learning models.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper proposes a method that uses machine learning to interpolate future weather in mountainous regions, improving forecasts by utilizing data from surrounding plains and past observations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method partially achieves improvements in RMSE with significantly less training time despite using a small dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method partially achieves improvements in RMSE with significantly less training time despite using a small dataset.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method provides maximum feature-label consistency of the transformed space and preserves the geometry of the original data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The projection matrix constructed using the proposed method is proved to be a JL-embedding.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed image-text alignment module SelfAlign improves retrieval accuracy while maintaining retrieval efficiency without extra supervision.

alignments.pub_claim_text: SelfAlign boosts the accuracy of state-of-the-art non-pre-training independent-embedding models by 9.1, 4.2, and 6.6 in terms of R@sum score on the Flickr30 K, MS-COCO 1 K, and MS-COCO 5 K datasets, respectively.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The retrieval accuracy of SelfAlign outperforms most existing interactive-embedding models while achieving a significant decrease in retrieval time.

alignments.pub_claim_text: An interactive user-adjustable parameter allows users to focus on weighted perspectives of interest and enables a smooth visual transition between varying perspectives.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The application potential of the proposed method is illustrated in model evaluation and visual-interactive labeling.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A comparative analysis is performed to evaluate the dimensionality reduction results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of the best performing model architecture to the cardiovascular disease treatment cohort resulted in a significant increase in performance.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Next-generation reservoir computing requires less than 10 times shorter 'warmup' time compared to traditional reservoir computing.
alignments.rationale: The publication significantly reduces the claimed warm-up speedup (from 1000x to <10x), representing a clear revision and narrowing of the original numeric claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Next-generation reservoir computing has an approximately 100 times higher accuracy in predicting the co-existing attractor characteristics compared to traditional reservoir computing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The next-generation reservoir computing approach has approximately 100 times higher accuracy in predicting the co-existing attractor characteristics compared to a traditional reservoir computer.
alignments.rationale: The large accuracy improvement claim is reproduced identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Next-generation reservoir computing predicts the basin of attraction with high accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Next-generation reservoir computing predicts the basin of attraction with high accuracy.
alignments.rationale: Prediction quality claim retained without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work supports the superior learning ability of next-generation reservoir computing for dynamical systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work supports the superior learning ability of the next-generation reservoir computing algorithm for dynamical systems.
alignments.rationale: The concluding statement about superior learning ability is present in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EHNet demonstrates an effective embedding adaptive-update strategy that maintains old knowledge and adapts to new classes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: EHNet demonstrates an effective embedding adaptive-update strategy that maintains old knowledge and adapts to new classes.
alignments.rationale: The core methodological claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hyper-class embedding learned by clustering category embeddings alleviates overfitting issues caused by few training samples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hyper-class embedding learned by clustering category embeddings alleviates overfitting issues caused by few training samples.
alignments.rationale: The mechanism and its effect on overfitting are stated identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments on PASCAL-5i and COCO datasets indicate that EHNet achieves new state-of-the-art performance in incremental few-shot semantic segmentation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments on PASCAL-5i and COCO datasets indicate that EHNet achieves new state-of-the-art performance in incremental few-shot semantic segmentation.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The designs of EHNet provide representation capability for classes with sufficient semantics and limited biases.
